content.problemStatement:
The weather report of Chefland is Good if the number of sunny days in a week is strictly greater than the number of rainy days.

Given 777 integers A1,A2,A3,A4,A5,A6,A7A_1, A_2, A_3, A_4, A_5, A_6, A_7A1​,A2​,A3​,A4​,A5​,A6​,A7​ where Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 denotes that the ithi^{th}ith day of week in Chefland is a sunny day, Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 denotes that the ithi^{th}ith day in Chefland is a rainy day. Determine if the weather report of Chefland is Good or not.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, 777 space separated integers A1,A2,A3,A4,A5,A6,A7A_1, A_2, A_3, A_4, A_5, A_6, A_7A1​,A2​,A3​,A4​,A5​,A6​,A7​.


Output Format
For each testcase, print "YES" if the weather report of Chefland is Good, otherwise print "NO". Print the output without quotes.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
constraints: Ai is either 0 or 1 for all i
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains 7 space‑separated integers A1 … A7, where Ai is 1 if the i‑th day is sunny and 0 if it is rainy.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the number of sunny days is strictly greater than the number of rainy days, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: sunny days = 5, rainy days = 2 → YES.
Test case 2: sunny days = 1, rainy days = 6 → NO.
Test case 3: sunny days = 7, rainy days = 0 → YES.
Test case 4: sunny days = 1, rainy days = 6 → NO.
First test case has 0 sunny days vs 7 rainy → NO. Second test case has 4 sunny vs 3 rainy → YES.
Sunny days = 3, rainy days = 4, so the report is not good.

solution.approach: For each test case count how many of the seven values are 1 (sunny days). The number of rainy days is 7 minus that count. If sunnyCount > rainyCount output YES, else NO. The algorithm is O(7) per test case, i.e., O(T) overall.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – we examine exactly 7 numbers per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        sunny = 0
        for _ in range(7):
            sunny += int(next(it))
        rainy = 7 - sunny
        out_lines.append('YES' if sunny > rainy else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
